methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $10M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $60M - $500M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

private.name: Tenna Systems
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Seed), post-money $60M

data.narrative:
Zeromark is a seed-stage defense and security company that has raised $10M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of 10 firms across the Defense & Security sector: 2 public companies (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private peers including Anduril Industries, Shield AI, and Flock Safety.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-Progression analysis used the $10M total raised as a proxy baseline and applied a typical 2.5x seed-to-next-stage step-up multiplier, yielding $25M. Peer-Median analysis examined five same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables: Stark Defence ($500M Series A), Vatn Systems ($300M Series A), Ares Industries ($150M Series A), NODA AI ($125M Series A), and Tenna Systems ($60M seed). The median across this peer set is $150M, with a range of $60M–$500M.

Triangulation of these two methods produces a valuation range of $21M to $172M, with a median point of $88M. The wide spread reflects material uncertainty around stage-appropriate valuation and peer relevance within the defense sector, where funding dynamics and customer relationships drive substantial variance. Stage progression implies a more conservative floor; peer medians suggest higher potential if Zeromark demonstrates comparable capabilities and traction to series-stage peers. Key uncertainties include product maturity, customer validation, and the weighting of seed-stage versus later-stage comparable firms.
